About: It is about a musical duo comprising singer-songwriter Paul Simon and singer Art Garfunkel.
ComposedBy: It was composed by Paul Simon.
ConsistsOf: It consists of Paul Simon and Art Garfunkel.
CreatedBy: It was created by Paul Simon and Art Garfunkel.
CreatedIn: It was created in Queens, New York City.
HasA: It has three U.S. number-one singles.
It has the Record of the Year Grammy winners "Mrs. Robinson" and "Bridge over Troubled Water".
It has sold more than 100 million records.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by being one of the best-selling musical acts of the 1960s.
It is characterized by being one of the world's best-selling albums.
It is characterized by one of the largest concert attendances in history.
It is characterized by being the most successful folk-rock duo of the 1960s.
It is characterized by being among the best-selling music artists.
HeldIn: It was held in Central Park.
IsA: It is an American musical duo.
It is one of the best-selling musical acts of the 1960s.
It is among the best-selling music artists.
KnownAs: It is known as Tom & Jerry.
KnownFor: It is known for "The Sound of Silence".
It is known for "Mrs. Robinson".
It is known for "Bridge over Troubled Water".
It is known for "Homeward Bound".
It is known for "I Am a Rock".
It is known for "Scarborough Fair/Canticle".
It is known for "A Hazy Shade of Winter".
It is known for "America".
It is known for "The Boxer".
It is known for "Cecilia".
MemberOf: It is a member of the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame.
OccurredAt: It occurred at Gerde's Folk City.
OccurredIn: It occurred in 1953.
It occurred in 1963.
It occurred in 1964.
It occurred in 1965.
It occurred in 1966.
It occurred in 1968.
It occurred in 1970.
It occurred in 1981.
It occurred in 1986.
It occurred in 1990.
ProducedBy: It was produced by Tom Wilson.
PublishedBy: It was published by Columbia Records.
It was published by Big Records.
It was published by Oriole label.
UsedIn: It is used in Mike Nichols's 1967 film The Graduate.
It is used in the Graduate soundtrack album.
It is used in the album Bookends.
It is used in the album Wednesday Morning, 3 A.M.
It is used in the album Sounds of Silence.
It is used in the album Parsley, Sage, Rosemary and Thyme.
It is used in the album Bridge over Troubled Water.
It is used in the album The Paul Simon Songbook.
WonBy: It was won by Art Garfunkel.
WrittenBy: It was written by Paul Simon.
